perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that the Confidential Information will be kept confidential and that Recipient and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Confidential Information may be kept reasonably confidential and that Recipient and its Representatives will endeavor not to disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Confidential Information 'will be kept confidential' and 'will not disclose', which creates a strong obligation. By changing it to 'may be kept reasonably confidential' and 'will endeavor not to disclose,' the obligation is weakened, and it is now open to interpretation what constitutes 'reasonable' and what actions constitute 'endeavor'. This change creates an internal contradiction with the rest of the agreement where strict confidentiality is presumed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) Use of Confidential Information . Recipient and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for no other purpose. Recipient acknowledges that the Confidential Information may be kept reasonably confidential and that Recipient and its Representatives will endeavor not to disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that (i) it may make any disclosure of the Confidential Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any of the Confidential Information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, who are bound by confidentiality obligations to Recipient or agree to keep such Confidential Information confidential to the same extent as Recipient. In any event, Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, to accept responsibility for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, and at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party understands that Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that may be similar to Disclosing Party’s information. Accordingly, this Agreement will not be construed as an obligation or representation that Recipient will not develop products or systems, or have products or systems developed for it, that compete with the products or systems contemplated by Disclosing Party’s information. In addition, the Disclosing Party agrees that Recipient does not intend to, and will not be obligated to, restrict or segregate the work assignments of personnel who may have been exposed to Disclosing Party’s information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Confidential Information 'will be kept confidential' and 'will not disclose'. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'may be kept reasonably confidential' and 'will endeavor not to disclose'. This introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation. The contradicted text uses both the original stricter phrasing and the relaxed phrasing, creating an in-text contradiction. While the change occurred within the contradicted text itself, the original language in the contradicted text contradicts the newly introduced weaker language, making the contract internally inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request (which request shall be made within a reasonable time after such termination of discussions) of the Disclosing Party for any reason, Recipient will, at Recipient’s option, either deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy (which destruction shall be certified in writing by an officer of Recipient) all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party
perturbation.changed_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request of the Disclosing Party, Recipient will, at Recipient’s convenience, endeavor to deliver to the Disclosing Party or possibly destroy all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates action after the termination of discussions. The terms 'at Recipient's option' and 'deliver or destroy' have been changed to 'at Recipient's convenience' and 'endeavor to deliver or possibly destroy'. This weakens the obligation and provides flexibility to the Recipient, leading to a contradiction with the confidentiality purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (5) Termination of Discussions . Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request of the Disclosing Party, Recipient will, at Recipient’s convenience, endeavor to deliver to the Disclosing Party or possibly destroy all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that Recipient and its Representatives (i) may each retain one copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for recordkeeping purposes and for the purposes of defending its rights and obligations hereunder and (ii) will not be required to return or destroy -2- any computer or other electronic hardware or systems, to render any electronic data irrecoverable or to disable or otherwise modify any existing electronic data backup procedures. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates action after termination. The changed text weakens this obligation by changing 'at Recipient’s option, either deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy' to 'at Recipient’s convenience, endeavor to deliver to the Disclosing Party or possibly destroy'. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which also contains the weakened obligation, because the change makes the action discretionary rather than obligatory, impacting the enforceability of the confidentiality agreement. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient understands and acknowledges that the Disclosing Party and its Representatives represent and warrant that the Disclosing Party has exercised reasonable care to ensure that all provided information is accurate.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that the Disclosing Party makes no warranties or representations regarding the information's accuracy. However, this has been changed to state that the Disclosing Party warrants that it has 'exercised reasonable care to ensure that all provided information is accurate', which contradicts the original premise of no warranty being provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) No Representation of Accuracy . Recipient understands and acknowledges that the Disclosing Party and its Representatives represent and warrant that the Disclosing Party has exercised reasonable care to ensure that all provided information is accurate. Recipient agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to Recipient or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Confidential Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties that are made in a final definitive agreement regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that there are no representations or warranties regarding the accuracy of the confidential information. The changed text introduces a warranty that the Disclosing Party has exercised reasonable care to ensure accuracy. This directly contradicts the original 'no warranty' position and therefore creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the Contradicted Text also reflects this, and the change makes it untrue. Thus, it is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties also agree that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the Company nor Nuance will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties acknowledge that unless and until a letter of intent regarding a Possible Transaction has been written, neither the Company nor Nuance will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates that a 'final definitive agreement' is required to establish legal obligations beyond what's in the confidentiality agreement. This has been changed to a 'letter of intent'. A letter of intent is generally non-binding, whereas a definitive agreement is legally binding. This creates confusion over when legal obligations arise, contradicting the intent of a confidentiality agreement that aims to protect information before a binding agreement is in place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (7) Definitive Agreements . Both parties understand and agree that no contract or agreement providing for any Possible Transaction shall be deemed to exist between Nuance and the Company unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered. Both parties acknowledge that unless and until a letter of intent regarding a Possible Transaction has been written, neither the Company nor Nuance will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other or any of its Representatives with regard to a Possible Transaction, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires a 'final definitive agreement' for legal obligations, while the changed text requires a 'letter of intent'. The contradicted text includes both the requirement for a 'final definitive agreement' and the acknowledgement of the 'letter of intent'. Because a letter of intent is generally non-binding, this contradicts the original requirement for a legally binding 'final definitive agreement', creating a direct conflict regarding when legal obligations arise. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the change introduces conflicting obligations regarding the establishment of legal obligations.

perturbation.original_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including an injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood that money damages may be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives and that the Disclosing Party may be entitled to monetary relief, as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that monetary damages would *not* be sufficient and grants the Disclosing Party the right to equitable relief (injunction). The modified version states that monetary damages *may* be sufficient, and the Disclosing Party *may* be entitled to monetary relief. The original remedy was equitable and immediate, while the modified version is monetary. This contradicts the original statement and changes the Disclosing Party's right to immediate action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (10) Injunctive Relief . It is understood that money damages may be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives and that the Disclosing Party may be entitled to monetary relief, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by the Recipient of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that money damages would *not* be a sufficient remedy, while the changed text states that they *may* be sufficient. This directly contradicts the statement in the Contradicted Text, which is identical to the Changed Text, creating an in-text contradiction. The original text granted the Disclosing Party the right to equitable relief, while the changed text gives it the possibility of monetary relief which the original text rejects. The change introduces conflicting statements about the sufficiency of money damages and the type of relief available, leading to confusion and unenforceability. The Change causes a contradiction to the contradicted text.
